subject: Human Resources
title: Family and Medical Leave Act
description: A quick, practical guide to Family and Medical Leave Act.

hook.text: A new father needs time off for his premature baby. Without FMLA, his job would be gone when he returned.

buildup.text: FMLA provides up to 12 weeks of unpaid, job-protected leave for birth, adoption, or serious health conditions.

discovery.text: It covers employers with 50+ employees and workers who've been employed at least 12 months with 1,250 hours worked.

twist.text: FMLA is unpaid—many workers can't afford to take it. The protection exists, but financial reality makes it inaccessible.

climax.text: Some states have passed paid family leave—filling the gap FMLA left by providing wage replacement during leave.

punchline.text: FMLA protects the job. Paid leave protects the person.

quiz.question: What is a major limitation of FMLA?
options: The leave is unpaid, making it financially inaccessible for many
It provides too much paid leave
